text.title: api-versioning Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for api-versioning as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Creating v2 by copying the entire v1 handler file and editing the copy.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Both copies diverge immediately. Bug fixes applied to v2 never reach v1. Shared behavior must live in a service layer, with handlers only handling version-specific formatting.

failure-mode.description: Introducing a new API version without adding Deprecation, Sunset, and Link headers to old version responses.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Without machine-readable deprecation signals, client developers only learn about sunsets when their integration breaks. This creates support emergencies.

failure-mode.description: Using ?v=1 or ?version=2 query parameters for public API versioning.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Query parameters invalidate CDN cache keys, cause logging blind spots, and are easily missed in SDK generation. Reserve query params for feature flags, not versions.

failure-mode.description: Using header-based versioning (Accept-Version) without adding Vary: Accept-Version to responses.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: CDNs cache based on URL by default. Without Vary, v1 and v2 responses for the same URL are served interchangeably, causing cross-version response contamination.

failure-mode.description: Creating a new API version when the change is purely additive (new optional field, new endpoint).
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Version bumps consume the support window budget. Additive changes are backward-compatible by definition. Signal them via OpenAPI changelog, not a new version.

failure-mode.description: Scheduling API version sunset based on time elapsed rather than measured consumer usage.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Consumers who call an API infrequently (monthly billing runs, annual reports) are invisible in daily traffic but break catastrophically on sunset. Require traffic-data approval for any sunset decision.
